source: CFPB Consumer Response Annual Report 2025, Section 4.1, Pages 16-20

top_issues.issue: Problem with investigation into existing issue

common_complaint_themes: Tradeline info errors (account status, balances, payment history)
Consumers denied jobs and apartments due to errors
Identity theft claims with police reports or FTC ID theft reports
company_response_patterns: NCRAs treated complaints as disputes or block requests
Responded with how info was verified, updated, removed, or not reported
Some said they tried to contact consumer but couldn't reach them
For improper use complaints, cited FCRA permissions, refused to delete inquiries

system_gaming_factors.finfluencers: Promoting complaint filing on social media (FinTok)
system_gaming_factors.ai_agents: Large language models and autonomous software systems flooding system

key_insight: 40% of credit reporting complaints result in corrections (non-monetary relief). If 40% require fixes, upstream data quality is broken.
